title: Crafting+
body:

[![github](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@intergrav/devins-badges@3/assets/compact/available/github_vector.svg)](https://github.com/Kxffie/CraftingPlus)
![fabric](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@intergrav/devins-badges@3/assets/compact/supported/fabric_vector.svg)

# Hey! I'm Kxffie!
Currently being worked on, will add whatever I feel is an upgrade.

You can find this datapack at https://modrinth.com/datapack/crafting+

# Features 319 Recipes (v1.21.8) 
<details open>
<summary>All features</summary>

# Recipes

## Blocks

### Bulk

#### Blasting - Blast Furnace Recipes

- Raw Copper Block to Copper Block
- Raw Gold Block to Gold Block
- Raw Iron Block to Iron Block

#### Smelting - Furnace Recipes

- Raw Copper Block to Copper Block
- Raw Gold Block to Gold Block
- Raw Iron Block to Iron Block

#### Blocks - Random Bulk Recipes

- Cauldron with Iron Blocks
- Chains with Iron Blocks and Ingots
- Chests with Logs
- Ladders with Logs
- Light & Heavy Weighted Pressure Plates with Gold/Iron Blocks

#### Items - Random Bulk Items

- Bowl with Logs
- Bread with Haybales
- Ender Eyes with Ender Pearls and Blaze Rods
- Sticks with Logs

### Buttons

- Using 1 material that can create a button and recieve 4 in return

### Convert

- Convert Glowberries to Glowstone
- Convert Dead Bushes to Sticks

### Custom Crafts

- Bookshelves using any book types
- Red Mushroom blocks from Red Mushrooms
- Brown Mushroom blocks from Brown Mushrooms
- Crimson Nylium/Warped Nylium
- Netherwart Block from Crimson Fungus
- Warped block from Warped Fungus
- Twisting Vines
- Weeping Vines
- Enchanted Golden Apples
- Webs
- Grass Blocks
- Rooted Dirt
- Sculk
- Sculk Shrieker
- Sculk Sensor
- Sculk Catalyst
- Shroomlights
- Nametags

### Flowers - Duplicate with Bonemeal in Crafting Table/Grid

#### Flower

- Any flower with Bonemeal

#### Leaves

- Duplicate Cherry Leaves, Flowering Azalea Leaves, Flowering Azalea, and Mangrove Propagule
- Duplicate Firefly Bush

#### Plants

- Carrots
- Potatos
- Beetroot
- Cactus
- Bamboo
- Sugarcane
- Lily Pads
- Sea Pickle
- Sweet Berries
- Glow Berries
- Chorus Fruit

#### Misc

- Cactus Flower
- Chorus Flower
- Pink Petals
- Pitcher Plant
- Spore Blossom
- Wildflowers
- Big Dripleaf
- Small Dripleaf

### Ores

#### Stone

- Craft all stone ore blocks with 5 stone and 4 ores/ingots

#### Deepslate

- Craft all deepslate ore blocks with 5 deepslate and 4 ores/ingots

#### Nether

- Craft all nether ore blocks with 5 netherrack and 4 ores/ingots

### Slabs back to Blocks

- Turn your stacks of slabs back into their block for by putting them left/right of eachother in a crafting grid.

## Color Variants

### Beds

- Recolor any bed by just putting it next to another dye in a crafting grid.

### Carpets

- Use any 8 carpets and surround by a dye to redye those carpets into the new dye color. 

### Shulkers

- Why craft your shulker box and then dye it? Just add the dye while crafting the shulker box!

### Wool

- Redye any wool by just putting it next to another dye in a crafting grid.

### Dyes - New Dye Mixes

- Smelt Coal to recieve Black dye
- Mix Orange and Black dye to get 2 Brown dye
- Alternitavely, you can mix 2 Red dye, 1 Yellow dye, and 1 Blue dye to create 4 Brown Dye
- Put Dried Kelp in a crafting grid to recieve Green dye
- Smelt a Moss block to recieve Green dye
- Put a Flowering Azalea or Flowering Azalea Leaves in a crafting grid to get Magenta Dye
- Put a Popped Chorus Fruit in a crafting grid to recieve Magenta Dye
- Use a Spore Blossom in a crafting grid to get Pink dye
- Whats that? Another crafting grid recipe? Yup, just put an Amethyst Shard in a crafting grid to get Purple dye
- Finally, something new, smelt Sweet Berries to get Red dye
- Use black dye in place of an ink sac in the Book and Quill recipe

## Functional

- Use any stone-type slabs in place of the slabs in the Armor Stand and Grindstone recipes
- Mix and match all obsidian and glass types in the Beacon recipe
- Mix any obsidian type in the enchant table recipe!
- Use any cobblestone-related blocks in place of the Brewing Stand recipe
- Use any cobblestone-related blocks in place of the Dispenser, Dropper, Brewing Stand, Furnace, Lever, Observer, Piston, Repeater and Comparator recipes! 

## Smelting

- Smelt all slabs and stair types that their full block counterpart can smelt into, such as cobblestone to stone, sandstone to smooth sandstone etc

## Special

- Put a tnt, furnace, hopper, or chest inside a minecart recipe to precraft into its respectable counterpart.

## Tools

- Craft all stone tools with all cobblestone-related blocks.
- Craft Spectral Arrows using more glowing related items!

## Uncrafting

### Blocks

- Amethyst to Shards
- Blue ice to Packed ice
- Brown Mushroom block to Brown Mushroom
- Red Mushroom block to Red Mushroom
- Calibrated Sensor to Sculk Sensor
- Clay block to Clay
- Cobweb to String
- Dripstone block to Pointed Dripstone
- 3 Gravel to 1 Flint
- 1 Honey block around 4 empty bottles to make 4 Honey Bottles
- Honeycomb Block to 4 honeycomb
- Magma Block to 4 Magma Cream
- Melon Block to 9 Melons
- Nether Brick Block to 4 Nether Bricks
- Nether Wart Block to 9 Nether Wart
- Warped Wart Block to 4 Warped Fungus
- Packed Ice to Ice
- Quartz Block to Quartz
- Sandstone to Sand
- Snow Block to Snowballs
- Wool to String

# Tags

## Item (What are Tags?)

Tags are a way of telling a recipe to allow more than one type of item. For example, for stone tools, I gave it my custom "Stone" tag, which means you can make a Stone Sword out of anything from the Stone list down below.

- Books (book, enchanted book, writable book, written book)
- Dirt (dirt, coarse dirt, rooted dirt, mud)
- Glass (all glass block colors)
- Glowing (glowstone dust, glow berries, glow ink sac, glow lichen)
- Grass (tall grass, short grass, tall dry grass, short dry grass, moss carpet, fern, seagrass)
- Mushroom (red & brown mushroom block, mushroom stem)
- Obsidian (obsidian and crying obsidian)
- Stone Slabs (all non-wooden slabs)
- Stone (stone, cobblestone, granite, andesite, diorite, tuff, cobbled deepslate, endstone, blackstone, netherrack, mossy cobblestone)

</details>
